terms.definition: Partial weakness affecting one side of the body, often following a stroke.
terms.exampleSentence: Right-sided hemiparesis limited his ability to grasp objects with the affected hand.
terms.contextNotes: Distinct from hemiplegia (complete paralysis); document MRC grade.

terms.definition: Complete paralysis of one side of the body, typically of cerebral origin.
terms.exampleSentence: Dense left hemiplegia was noted on admission following a right MCA infarct.
terms.contextNotes: Often co-occurs with neglect, dysphasia and visual field defects.

terms.definition: Impaired coordination of voluntary movement, usually due to cerebellar dysfunction.
terms.exampleSentence: Truncal ataxia made independent sitting balance unsafe without support.
terms.contextNotes: Tested with finger-nose, heel-shin and tandem gait.

terms.definition: Velocity-dependent increase in muscle tone associated with upper motor neurone lesions.
terms.exampleSentence: Spasticity in the calf restricted ankle dorsiflexion during gait.
terms.contextNotes: Graded with Modified Ashworth Scale; managed with stretching, splinting, botulinum toxin.

terms.definition: Neurodevelopmental treatment aimed at normalising tone and re-learning movement post-stroke.
terms.exampleSentence: The Bobath approach was used to facilitate normal sit-to-stand patterns.
terms.contextNotes: Modern Bobath emphasises task-specific practice, not just tone normalisation.

terms.definition: Movement patterns and stretching using diagonal patterns to enhance neuromuscular control.
terms.exampleSentence: Proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation diagonals were used to retrain upper limb function.
terms.contextNotes: Common patterns: D1 and D2 flexion/extension for upper and lower limb.

terms.definition: Positioning that uses gravity to clear secretions from specific lung segments.
terms.exampleSentence: Postural drainage in side-lying with the head down assisted clearance of the right lower lobe.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid head-down tilt with raised ICP, severe reflux or unstable cardiovascular status.

terms.definition: Cycle of breathing control, thoracic expansion and forced expiration to mobilise secretions.
terms.exampleSentence: Active cycle of breathing techniques produced a productive cough and clearer auscultation.
terms.contextNotes: Cycle: breathing control, 3-4 thoracic expansions, breathing control, FET (huffs).

terms.definition: Rhythmical clapping over the chest wall with cupped hands to loosen retained secretions.
terms.exampleSentence: Manual percussion was performed over the right lower zone for five minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid over fractures, surgical wounds, recent haemoptysis or low platelets.

terms.definition: Use of a handheld device to encourage deep, sustained inspirations post-operatively.
terms.exampleSentence: Incentive spirometry was prescribed hourly to prevent post-operative atelectasis.
terms.contextNotes: Aim for sustained maximal inspiration with 3-5 second hold.

terms.definition: Maximum expiratory flow rate, used to monitor airway calibre in asthma and COPD.
terms.exampleSentence: Her peak flow improved from 220 to 310 L/min after bronchodilator therapy.
terms.contextNotes: Best of three attempts; technique-dependent.

terms.term: forced expiratory volume in one second
terms.definition: Volume of air forcibly exhaled in the first second of a maximal expiration.
terms.exampleSentence: Her forced expiratory volume in one second was 65% of predicted, suggesting moderate obstruction.
terms.contextNotes: FEV1/FVC < 0.7 indicates obstructive pattern.

terms.definition: Walking aids that transfer load through the upper limbs to offload a lower limb.
terms.exampleSentence: She was discharged on elbow crutches with partial weight-bearing on the right leg.
terms.contextNotes: Document weight-bearing status (NWB, PWB, FWB) and stair-climbing competence.

terms.definition: Four-legged metal frame providing wide-based support for unsteady walkers.
terms.exampleSentence: A walking frame was issued to improve safety following her fall.
terms.contextNotes: Adjust handle height to wrist crease with arm relaxed.

terms.definition: Wheeled walking frame with brakes and a seat, used by patients needing dynamic support.
terms.exampleSentence: A four-wheeled rollator allowed her to mobilise outdoors with rest stops.
terms.contextNotes: Teach safe braking before use outdoors or on slopes.

terms.definition: Pair of fixed handrails used for supported standing and gait re-education.
terms.exampleSentence: Initial gait re-education was performed in the parallel bars to ensure safety.
terms.contextNotes: Adjust bar height to greater trochanter; ensure non-slip surface.

terms.definition: Elastic band used to provide graded resistance during strengthening exercises.
terms.exampleSentence: A red resistance band was used for shoulder external rotation exercises.
terms.contextNotes: Colour codes vary by brand; always note brand and resistance level.

terms.definition: Round balance platform used to challenge proprioception and ankle stability.
terms.exampleSentence: Single-leg standing on a wobble board progressed her ankle rehabilitation.
terms.contextNotes: Begin double-leg with hand support before progressing to single-leg eyes-closed.

terms.definition: Stationary cycling ergometer used for cardiovascular conditioning and lower-limb rehabilitation.
terms.exampleSentence: Ten minutes on the exercise bike preceded his strengthening programme.
terms.contextNotes: Set saddle height so knee is in 25-30° flexion at bottom of stroke.

terms.definition: Adjustable treatment couch on which patients are positioned for assessment and therapy.
terms.exampleSentence: She was positioned in supine on the plinth for hip range of motion testing.
terms.contextNotes: Adjust to therapist waist height to protect own back.

terms.definition: Movement of a patient from one surface to another, e.g., bed to chair.
terms.exampleSentence: She required one assist for a stand-pivot transfer from bed to wheelchair.
terms.contextNotes: Document level of assistance: independent, supervision, 1× or 2× assist.

terms.definition: Mechanical device used to lift and move dependent patients between surfaces.
terms.exampleSentence: A full-body hoist was required for transfers due to dense hemiplegia.
terms.contextNotes: Always use the correct sling size; check loop colour-coding before lifting.

terms.definition: External brace that controls ankle and foot position to assist gait and prevent deformity.
terms.exampleSentence: An ankle-foot orthosis was prescribed to manage foot drop after his stroke.
terms.contextNotes: Check skin daily for pressure marks; review fit as oedema changes.

terms.definition: Mobility device with wheels used by patients unable to walk independently.
terms.exampleSentence: A self-propelling wheelchair was issued for community mobility.
terms.contextNotes: Ensure correct seat width and pressure-relieving cushion.

terms.definition: Device that converts liquid medication into a fine mist for inhalation.
terms.exampleSentence: A salbutamol nebuliser was given before the chest physiotherapy session.
terms.contextNotes: Often timed before chest clearance to maximise airway calibre.
